prompt_sent:
You are the Distiller skill. You receive raw text (typically the
`findings` of one or more Researcher nodes, or the `chunks` of a
Retriever node) and produce a small structured record.

You make no tool calls. You do no web access. Everything you need is
already in the prompt under INPUTS.

Procedure:
  1. Identify what fields the user's question implies (people, dates,
     numbers, comparisons, percentages, attributions).
  2. Pull those fields out of the inputs.
  3. Emit a compact JSON record. Fields with no evidence in the inputs
     are omitted, not made up.

Output schema (JSON, no prose, no markdown fences):

  {
    "fields": { "<field_name>": "<value>", ... },
    "rationale": "<one short sentence saying which input supports each field>"
  }

Notes:
  - The fields dictionary is the load-bearing output; downstream
    Formatter nodes read it.
  - When the question is a comparison (`fastest growing`, `largest`),
    emit a `comparison` key with `winner: <id>` and `reason: <short>`.
  - When the question's evidence is missing, set `fields: {}` and put
    the gap in `rationale`. Do not invent.
  - URL RESOLUTION: When you extract URLs from page text and they are
    RELATIVE (start with `../`, `./`, or `/`), you MUST resolve them
    to ABSOLUTE URLs by combining with the source page's base URL.
    The source URL is visible in the INPUTS block (look for the
    upstream browser node's `url` field). For example:
      relative: `../../../sapiens_996/index.html`
      source:   `https://books.toscrape.com/catalogue/category/books/history_32/index.html`
      absolute: `https://books.toscrape.com/catalogue/sapiens_996/index.html`
    Always output absolute `https://...` URLs in your fields.
  - When extracting a LIST of items (books, products, models), emit
    them under a single `items` key as a JSON array of objects, each
    with uniform field names. Example:
      "items": [
        {"title": "...", "url": "https://...", "rating": "Five", "price": "£54.23"},
        {"title": "...", "url": "https://...", "rating": "Four", "price": "£32.10"}
      ]

A Critic node may run after you. Its evaluation will fail if you
invented fields or made claims unsupported by the inputs.

QUESTION: Extract structured data for comparison: course name, provider, fees (in INR), duration, ratings/feedback, registration availability in next 3 months, and value-for-money assessment

INPUTS:
[
  {
    "id": "n:4",
    "kind": "upstream",
    "skill": "browser",
    "output": {
      "url": "https://www.edureka.co/masters-program/generative-ai-prompt-engineering-training",
      "goal": "extract course fees, duration, ratings, feedback, enrollment dates, and registration deadlines from this course page",
      "path": "extract",
      "turns": 0,

Overview and key features of the Gen AI Masters Program 	
Edureka’s Generative AI Masters Program is designed to help learners build practical expertise in Generative AI, Agentic AI, Prompt Engineering, and AI-powered applications. The program combines live instructor-led training with self-paced courses, hands-on projects, and real-world use cases to prepare learners for emerging AI roles.
Who should enroll in this online Gen AI training course? 	
This Gen AI Masters program is ideal for software developers, data professionals, AI enthusiasts, business professionals, students, and working professionals looking to build practical skills in Generative AI and modern AI workflows.
What topics are included in the Generative AI master program syllabus? 	
The Gen AI Masters program covers Python for AI, Artificial Intelligence fundamentals, Prompt Engineering, Agentic AI, AI for Business Transformation, AI-assisted software development, productivity tools using ChatGPT, and industry-focused Generative AI applications.
Are there any prerequisites for joining this Gen AI course? 	
There are no prerequisites for enrolling in this Gen AI course, anyone interested can join!
Is this Gen AI certification training instructor-led or self-paced? 	
The program includes a combination of live instructor-led courses and self-paced learning modules to provide flexibility and practical learning experience.
Why Learn Generative AI in 2026?
Generative AI is one of the fastest-growing fields, with strong demand across technology, healthcare, finance, marketing, and automation, making it a valuable career skill in 2026.
How much does this Generative AI training program cost? 	
The course fee may vary based on ongoing offers, learning plans, and region. Please visit the Edureka website or contact support for the latest pricing details.
Why This Generative AI Course vs Other Programs?
This Generative AI course combines LLMs, Prompt Engineering, RAG, AI Agents, hands-on projects, and career support, helping learners build real-world AI applications rather than just learning theory.
What is the typical duration for completing the online Gen AI courses? 	
The Generative AI Masters Program includes 8 industry-focused courses with hands-on projects and labs. Most learners complete the program within 4–6 months based on their learning pace.
What are the eligibility criteria for becoming a certified AI professional? 	
There are no strict eligibility requirements. Anyone interested in learning Generative AI, AI workflows, and modern AI applications can enroll in the program.
Do I receive certification after completing the training? 	
Yes, once you complete the Gen AI Online Course, you will receive a lifetime-valid course completion certificate.
Is this Gen AI masters program suitable for beginners or those new to AI? 	
Yes. The program includes beginner-friendly modules that introduce AI concepts, prompt engineering, and Agentic AI step-by-step through practical examples and guided learning.
Which programming languages are commonly used in AI development? 	
The programming languages commonly used in generative AI development are Python, R, Julia, and JavaScript.
Can you provide a brief introduction to generative AI technologies? 	
Generative AI is a type of artificial intelligence that uses advanced algorithms and neural networks to create new, original content such as text, images, or audio based on patterns learned from existing data.
What roles and responsibilities do AI engineers typically have? 	
A Generative AI Engineer designs, develops, trains, and optimizes AI models, prepares and processes data, evaluates model performance, integrates solutions into systems, and collaborates across teams to deliver effective AI applications.
Why should I become a Generative AI Engineer? 	
You should become a Generative AI Engineer because it offers exciting career opportunities, high salary potential, and the chance to work at the forefront of innovative AI technologies transforming industries worldwide.
What career opportunities are available after completing a Gen AI Masters Program? 	
After completing a Gen AI Masters Program, you can pursue roles like AI Research Scientist, Machine Learning Engineer, Generative AI Engineer, Data Scientist, AI Product Manager, Deep Learning Engineer, AI Consultant, AI Content Creator, and creative or technical specialist positions across industries such as tech, healthcare, finance, and entertainment
Is prior coding experience required for the certification courses? 	
Yes, coding skills are generally needed for this Gen AI certification course, especially knowledge of Python. You will use programming languages and key libraries like TensorFlow and PyTorch to build, train, and optimize generative AI models effectively. Coding lets you turn AI algorithms into real-world applications and customize models to meet specific needs.
Why is AI becoming important across various industries? 	
Generative AI is important across industries because it automates complex tasks, enhances creativity, personalizes customer experiences, and drives efficiency, enabling businesses to innovate and compete effectively
What is the expected salary range for AI engineering roles? 	
According to Glassdoor, Generative AI Engineers can earn ₹4 to 15+ LPA in India and $90,000 to $100,000+ annually in the US, depending on experience and expertise.
Can this AI master program be taken alongside other instructor-led courses? 	
Yes, you can take this AI master program along with other instructor-led or self-paced
